mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing equity risk premiums and supporting sustained S&P 500 appreciation over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over any 12-month rolling window, or correlation between the composite index and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects expanding corporate capacity utilization and near-term earnings momentum, producing immediate positive S&P 500 returns within a one-week window during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month changes during late-cycle periods, or the signal generates losses in over 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.5 percent validates corporate profit expansion expectations, leading to systematic positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient remains negative or statistically insignificant across all rolling 3-year periods, or forward returns do not differ meaningfully from the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within a one-week trading window.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of inflation spikes, or forward returns show no negative skew relative to low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rate confirms structural labor market healing, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The composite labor health index fails to predict forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in retail sales and food services revenue directly reduces corporate top-line growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month specifically during demand-shock regimes.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
